target.title: perf(ci): use oxlint for simple boundary guards
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`CI / check-additional-boundaries-a` spends time launching TypeScript-backed guard scripts for simple syntax bans. Two of those guards only need focused lint rules:

- deprecated `registerHttpHandler` usage in `src` and bundled plugin code
- raw `window.open` / `globalThis.open` usage outside the safe UI helper

## Why This Change Was Made

This replaces those two bespoke TypeScript guard scripts with focused Oxlint configs. The package scripts now route through `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --openclaw-focused-config`, so they keep wrapper-owned sparse-target filtering and managed execution while avoiding broad type-aware/unused-disable/artifact/lock work that is unrelated to these single-rule guards.

Behavior note: Oxlint's `eslint/no-restricted-properties` flags property access, while the old scripts flagged calls. I checked the current codebase for property-only false positives:

- `registerHttpHandler` property non-calls: `0`
- `window.open` / `globalThis.open` property non-calls: `0`

If a legitimate future property-only reference is needed, it can be excluded locally with a targeted Oxlint directive, for example:

```ts
// oxlint-disable-next-line eslint/no-restricted-properties -- legitimate property-only reference
const openRef = window.open;
```

The same targeted directive works for a legitimate `registerHttpHandler` property-only reference.

## User Impact

No product behavior change. This only reduces CI guard overhead.

## What Gets Optimized

`CI / check-additional-boundaries-a` runs both optimized checks. Fresh 3-run timing measured before commit on June 20, 2026:

| Check | Original TS guard baseline | Final wrapper-focused Oxlint | Final delta |
| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `lint:plugins:no-register-http-handler` | 6.404s, 6.508s, 6.835s, avg 6.58s | 1.006s, 1.467s, 1.562s, avg 1.35s | ~5.24s saved/run |
| `lint:ui:no-raw-window-open` | 0.575s, 1.000s, 1.288s, avg 0.95s | 1.008s, 0.911s, 1.019s, avg 0.98s | ~0.03s slower/run |

Combined `check-additional-boundaries-a` delta: original avg 7.54s -> final avg 2.32s, about 5.21s saved/run.

The 30d CI report estimates `CI / check-additional-boundaries-a` runs 36,902 times/month. At ~5.21s saved/run, this is about 192,342s/month, or ~53.4 runner-hours/month saved.

Conservative lower bound from exact mapped PR-head check runs: 433 runs in 30d, about 37.6 minutes/month saved for that exact mapped subset.

Wrapper repair timing also shows no regression versus the first direct-Oxlint version: direct package scripts before the wrapper repair averaged 1.41s and 1.04s; final wrapper-focused package scripts averaged 1.35s and 0.98s.

## Evidence

Focused validation passed: `pnpm -s run lint:plugins:no-register-http-handler`, `pnpm -s run lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/oxlint-config.test.ts`, and `git diff --check`. Detailed behavior proof is included below.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `check-additional-boundaries-a` used TypeScript-backed scripts for two simple forbidden-API checks; this PR moves those checks to focused Oxlint configs behind the repo Oxlint wrapper.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, using the committed package scripts, Oxlint configs, and wrapper fast path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm -s run lint:plugins:no-register-http-handler`
  2. `pnpm -s run lint:ui:no-raw-window-open`
  3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts test/scripts/oxlint-config.test.ts`
  4. `git diff --check`
  5. Rebuilt the deleted `scripts/check-no-raw-window-open.mjs` file collector and compared it to `pnpm exec oxlint --config config/oxlint/no-raw-window-open.json --debug=files ui/src/ui`.
  6. Ran the current-code false-positive scan for property-only references.
  7. Ran `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --prompt-file <evidence> --parallel-tests <focused proof>`.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Both focused lint commands passed through `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --openclaw-focused-config`.
  - Wrapper/config tests passed: 2 files, 42 tests.
  - The window-open file-selection comparison produced 223 old effective files and 223 Oxlint files, with no only-old or only-Oxlint paths.
  - `ui/src/ui/open-external-url.ts` still contains the intentional `window.open(...)` call, and `pnpm -s run lint:ui:no-raw-window-open` passes.
  - Current-code false-positive scan found `0` `registerHttpHandler` property non-calls and `0` `window.open` / `globalThis.open` property non-calls.
  - Autoreview was clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result after fix: the combined fresh measured delta is ~5.21s saved per `check-additional-boundaries-a` run, which projects to ~53.4 runner-hours/month from the 30d CI report estimate.
- What was not tested: full CI was not run locally; PR CI remains the authoritative broad check.
target.author: zats
